title: [InstCombine] Loosen one-use restriction if common operand is constant
summary:
The generalization extends the `(A | B) ^ (A | C) → (B ^ C) & ~A` pattern by relaxing the one-use restriction when the common operand `A` is a constant. Originally, the transformation required both `or` instructions to have only one use, limiting applicability. The patch adds a second, more permissive check that matches the same pattern without the one-use constraint, but only when `A` is an immediate constant. This is safe because constants can be freely duplicated without increasing instruction count or register pressure.

Key steps:
1. Keep the original one-use variant for general cases.
2. Add a new constant-specific variant using `m_ImmConstant()` to identify constants.
3. Use `m_c_Or` for commutative matching to cover all operand orderings.
4. Apply the transformation directly using `Builder.CreateNot` and `CreateXor`.

This approach balances optimization power with compile-time cost by expanding patterns only when profitable (constants). Developers can apply similar logic to other transformations by first identifying safe-to-duplicate operands (like constants) and lifting one-use restrictions conditionally.
